{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608851]] Wikidata name: Miwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Miwa Shrine (美和神社) |image = File:美和神社 (長野市) 三ツ鳥居.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 |位置 = {{Coord|36|39|47.04|N|138|12|11.72|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=美和神社}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} |神体 = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]] |別名 = |札所等 = [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]] Seven Shrines |festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]] (Spring Grand Festival)
[[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]] (Autumn Grand Festival) |神事 = New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Miwa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=256|relief=1}} |prefecture = {{ill|Miwa|qid=Q11357343}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.66305555555556|N|138.20325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the Miwa area of Nagano City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} whose former Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also one of the seven Zenkoji shrines. == Overview == [[File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿斜め.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] It stands in front of a station in a residential area near the urban center of Nagano City, yet the shrine grounds remain quiet. A distinctive {{ill|Three Torii|ja|三ツ鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Torii|qq=|1=mitsutorii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354046}} (also known as {{ill|Miwa Torii|ja|三輪鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) marks the entrance, and nearly one hundred stone shrines (the precinct’s “Hyakusessha”) line the grounds. The New Year's Eve Festival, during which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in these stone monuments, is a singular {{ill|Special Ritual|ja|特殊神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} unlike any other. Festivals are held in the seven districts of Hongo, Ainomihigashi, Ainominishi, Yokoyama, Yodogahashi, Miwatanamachi, and Araya. == Deity == ; Main Deity * '''{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}''' (Ōmononushi-no-mikoto) :: It is identical with the deity of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]. It represents the {{ill|Japanese Spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and is said to be the father of Suiginami, the deity of {{ill|Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387216}}.‘Japan's Gods’ – Miwa Shrine entry. ; Associated Deities * {{ill|Kunari-hime|ja|国業比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunari-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kunari-himeno-kami) – said to be the mother of Ōmononushi.  Note: The mother of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], who is equated with Ōmononushi, is {{ill|Sashinakowakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinakowakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kamihato-no-kami|ja|神部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihato-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – also known as {{ill|Kamifuku-no-kami|ja|神服部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamifuku-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (read: kamihatori-no-kami).“Kamibukami”: as noted on the shrine’s bulletin board; “Kamifukami”: according to ‘Japan's Gods’ and ‘Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ – Miwa Shrine entry. == History == === Historical Overview === Its founding is unknown. Both the deity and the shrine’s name are identical to those of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], making it a shrine of the Miwa clan; however, its origins remain unclear. According to {{ill|Zenkoji Engi|ja|善光寺縁起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Engi|qq=|1=Zenkoji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116897988}}, it is recorded that Miwa Tokimaru, originally from Yamato Miwa, once visited Zenkoji and remained here, dedicating the sacred object of Ōmiwa Shrine at that time – which is why Ōmiwa Shrine does not possess a sacred object. This legend is also interpreted as evidence that members of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} migrated to this area; nearby there are sites known as Tokimaru’s Mound and Tokimaru-ji. Furthermore, pottery excavated from the {{ill|Matsunoyama Kiln Site|ja|松ノ山窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoyama Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Tanonoguchi, Shingara-cho, Nagano City, exhibits morphological and technical similarities to that from the {{ill|Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|ja|陶邑窯跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|qq=|1=Suemura Kiln Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658478}} in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]]. (This pottery is compared with the TK47 style—up to the MT15 style—dating it to the early 6th century according to the Izumi Tōyū Kiln chronicle.) As the Tōyū kilns are thought to have been operated by immigrants ([[:en:Toraijin|Immigrants]]) under the Miwa clan, this further suggests that the Miwa clan may have migrated to Nagano City.Yūki Mizoguchi, ‘Japan’s Ancient Regions and Social Integration’ (Yoshikawa Kōbun, 2015, On-Demand Edition, ISBN 9784642746182)Ken Kirihara, ‘My Notes on Ancient Studies’ (Shingai Shoseki Publishing Center, 1983) In literature, the earliest record appears in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), on February 7, when the associated deity Kunari-hime was granted a divine Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth ([[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Divine Rank]]) as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In Jōgan 8 ([[:en:866|866]]), to avert the calamity of an epidemic, an offering and sutra chanting was performed to the Sanwa deity and the Kamihato-no-kami.“Shingi-kan Sōgen. In Shinano Province, Suinai District, the Sanwa and the two Kamibu deities possessed a wrathful spirit that could cause epidemics. The provincial official and lecturer performed devout purification and offering, reciting a thousand volumes of the Diamond Sutra and ten thousand volumes of the Heart Sutra to appease the deities’ anger and avert the epidemic.” ([http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html ‘Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku’, complete edition Asahi Shimbun version]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}} ([http://www.j-texts.com/ J-TEXTS Japanese Electronic Library]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}})). This indicates the importance of the deity in the region. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as “Shinano Province, {{ill|Suinai District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinai District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, Miwa Shrine” and classifies it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Its former name was “Miwa Shrine” (三輪神社), but in An'ei 3 ([[:en:1774|1774]]), permission was granted by the Yoshida family to change its name to the current “Miwa Shrine”. In Kansei 5 ([[:en:1793|1793]]), the current shrine building was constructed. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6, [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1935 (Showa 10, [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]) it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1986 (Showa 61), it was designated as a shrine for offering envoys. === Divine Rank === * On February 7 in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), the associated deity Kunari-hime was conferred a Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth (recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the first appearance in literature. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine building was rebuilt in Kansei 5 (1793). File:美和神社 (長野市) 本殿.JPG|The upper structure of the main hall
The main hall is a single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]]. File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden (Worship Hall) * Norito Hall * Shrine Office == Auxiliary Shrines == File:美和神社 (長野市) 青麻神社.JPG|{{ill|Aoso Shrine|ja|青麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoso Shrine|qq=}}
During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], Tadaemon Osuga, a local resident, enshrined it in hopes of a speedy recovery from {{ill|Apoplexy|ja|中風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apoplexy|qq=}}. File:美和神社 (長野市) 境内の石祠群.JPG|Stone shrines of the precinct’s Hyakusessha
Along the boundary wall, stone shrines enshrining the nation’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] are arrayed, colloquially known as “Hyakusessha.” == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Exorcism Festival ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Spring Grand Festival ([[:en:April 27|April 27]]) * Wind Festival ([[:en:September 1|September 1]]) * Autumn Grand Festival ([[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]]) * Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) ([[:en:November 27|November 27]]) * New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) – A special ritual in which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in the stone shrines regarded as the nation’s Ichinomiya. * Ōharae (Grand Purification) ([[:en:December 31|December 31]]) == Local Information == ; Location * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 ; Transportation Access * Nearest Station: [[:en:Nagano Electric Railway|Nagano Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nagano Line|ja|長野電鉄長野線|lt=Nagano Line|lt_ja=長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Line|qq=|1=Nagano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8579636}}, [[:en:Hongō Station (Nagano)|Hongo Station (Nagano Prefecture)]] (a short walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine Grounds Information Board * ‘The System of Japanese Historical Place Names: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) – Miwa Shrine entry (Nagano City) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, ‘The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano’ ({{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}) – Miwa Shrine entry === Related Articles === ; Other Zenkoji Seven Shrines * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398887}} * {{ill|Kiryu Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Shrine|qq=|1=Kitome Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519306}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=|1=Yanagihara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534153}} (Sasayaki Shrine) [[ja:美和神社 (長野市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-179.html Miwa Shrine] (National Zenkoji Association) {{citation needed|ja|美和神社|version=103356498|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Miwa Shrine (長野市)]] [[ja:美和神社]] {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|version=102536211|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608851]] Wikidata name: Miwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Miwa Shrine (美和神社) |image = File:美和神社 (長野市) 三ツ鳥居.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 |位置 = {{Coord|36|39|47.04|N|138|12|11.72|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=美和神社}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} |神体 = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]] |別名 = |札所等 = [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]] Seven Shrines |festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]] (Spring Grand Festival)
[[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]] (Autumn Grand Festival) |神事 = New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Miwa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=256|relief=1}} |prefecture = {{ill|Miwa|qid=Q11357343}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.66305555555556|N|138.20325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the Miwa area of Nagano City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} whose former Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also one of the seven Zenkoji shrines. == Overview == [[File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿斜め.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] It stands in front of a station in a residential area near the urban center of Nagano City, yet the shrine grounds remain quiet. A distinctive {{ill|Three Torii|ja|三ツ鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Torii|qq=|1=mitsutorii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354046}} (also known as {{ill|Miwa Torii|ja|三輪鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) marks the entrance, and nearly one hundred stone shrines (the precinct’s “Hyakusessha”) line the grounds. The New Year's Eve Festival, during which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in these stone monuments, is a singular {{ill|Special Ritual|ja|特殊神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} unlike any other. Festivals are held in the seven districts of Hongo, Ainomihigashi, Ainominishi, Yokoyama, Yodogahashi, Miwatanamachi, and Araya. == Deity == ; Main Deity * '''{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}''' (Ōmononushi-no-mikoto) :: It is identical with the deity of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]. It represents the {{ill|Japanese Spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and is said to be the father of Suiginami, the deity of {{ill|Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387216}}.‘Japan's Gods’ – Miwa Shrine entry. ; Associated Deities * {{ill|Kunari-hime|ja|国業比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunari-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kunari-himeno-kami) – said to be the mother of Ōmononushi.  Note: The mother of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], who is equated with Ōmononushi, is {{ill|Sashinakowakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinakowakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kamihato-no-kami|ja|神部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihato-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – also known as {{ill|Kamifuku-no-kami|ja|神服部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamifuku-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (read: kamihatori-no-kami).“Kamibukami”: as noted on the shrine’s bulletin board; “Kamifukami”: according to ‘Japan's Gods’ and ‘Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ – Miwa Shrine entry. == History == === Historical Overview === Its founding is unknown. Both the deity and the shrine’s name are identical to those of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], making it a shrine of the Miwa clan; however, its origins remain unclear. According to {{ill|Zenkoji Engi|ja|善光寺縁起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Engi|qq=|1=Zenkoji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116897988}}, it is recorded that Miwa Tokimaru, originally from Yamato Miwa, once visited Zenkoji and remained here, dedicating the sacred object of Ōmiwa Shrine at that time – which is why Ōmiwa Shrine does not possess a sacred object. This legend is also interpreted as evidence that members of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} migrated to this area; nearby there are sites known as Tokimaru’s Mound and Tokimaru-ji. Furthermore, pottery excavated from the {{ill|Matsunoyama Kiln Site|ja|松ノ山窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoyama Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Tanonoguchi, Shingara-cho, Nagano City, exhibits morphological and technical similarities to that from the {{ill|Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|ja|陶邑窯跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|qq=|1=Suemura Kiln Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658478}} in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]]. (This pottery is compared with the TK47 style—up to the MT15 style—dating it to the early 6th century according to the Izumi Tōyū Kiln chronicle.) As the Tōyū kilns are thought to have been operated by immigrants ([[:en:Toraijin|Immigrants]]) under the Miwa clan, this further suggests that the Miwa clan may have migrated to Nagano City.Yūki Mizoguchi, ‘Japan’s Ancient Regions and Social Integration’ (Yoshikawa Kōbun, 2015, On-Demand Edition, ISBN 9784642746182)Ken Kirihara, ‘My Notes on Ancient Studies’ (Shingai Shoseki Publishing Center, 1983) In literature, the earliest record appears in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), on February 7, when the associated deity Kunari-hime was granted a divine Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth ([[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Divine Rank]]) as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In Jōgan 8 ([[:en:866|866]]), to avert the calamity of an epidemic, an offering and sutra chanting was performed to the Sanwa deity and the Kamihato-no-kami.“Shingi-kan Sōgen. In Shinano Province, Suinai District, the Sanwa and the two Kamibu deities possessed a wrathful spirit that could cause epidemics. The provincial official and lecturer performed devout purification and offering, reciting a thousand volumes of the Diamond Sutra and ten thousand volumes of the Heart Sutra to appease the deities’ anger and avert the epidemic.” ([http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html ‘Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku’, complete edition Asahi Shimbun version]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}} ([http://www.j-texts.com/ J-TEXTS Japanese Electronic Library]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}})). This indicates the importance of the deity in the region. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as “Shinano Province, {{ill|Suinai District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinai District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, Miwa Shrine” and classifies it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Its former name was “Miwa Shrine” (三輪神社), but in An'ei 3 ([[:en:1774|1774]]), permission was granted by the Yoshida family to change its name to the current “Miwa Shrine”. In Kansei 5 ([[:en:1793|1793]]), the current shrine building was constructed. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6, [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1935 (Showa 10, [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]) it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1986 (Showa 61), it was designated as a shrine for offering envoys. === Divine Rank === * On February 7 in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), the associated deity Kunari-hime was conferred a Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth (recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the first appearance in literature. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine building was rebuilt in Kansei 5 (1793). File:美和神社 (長野市) 本殿.JPG|The upper structure of the main hall
The main hall is a single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]]. File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden (Worship Hall) * Norito Hall * Shrine Office == Auxiliary Shrines == File:美和神社 (長野市) 青麻神社.JPG|{{ill|Aoso Shrine|ja|青麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoso Shrine|qq=}}
During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], Tadaemon Osuga, a local resident, enshrined it in hopes of a speedy recovery from {{ill|Apoplexy|ja|中風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apoplexy|qq=}}. File:美和神社 (長野市) 境内の石祠群.JPG|Stone shrines of the precinct’s Hyakusessha
Along the boundary wall, stone shrines enshrining the nation’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] are arrayed, colloquially known as “Hyakusessha.” == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Exorcism Festival ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Spring Grand Festival ([[:en:April 27|April 27]]) * Wind Festival ([[:en:September 1|September 1]]) * Autumn Grand Festival ([[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]]) * Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) ([[:en:November 27|November 27]]) * New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) – A special ritual in which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in the stone shrines regarded as the nation’s Ichinomiya. * Ōharae (Grand Purification) ([[:en:December 31|December 31]]) == Local Information == ; Location * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 ; Transportation Access * Nearest Station: [[:en:Nagano Electric Railway|Nagano Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nagano Line|ja|長野電鉄長野線|lt=Nagano Line|lt_ja=長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Line|qq=|1=Nagano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8579636}}, [[:en:Hongō Station (Nagano)|Hongo Station (Nagano Prefecture)]] (a short walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine Grounds Information Board * ‘The System of Japanese Historical Place Names: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) – Miwa Shrine entry (Nagano City) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, ‘The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano’ ({{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}) – Miwa Shrine entry === Related Articles === ; Other Zenkoji Seven Shrines * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398887}} * {{ill|Kiryu Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Shrine|qq=|1=Kitome Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519306}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=|1=Yanagihara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534153}} (Sasayaki Shrine) [[ja:美和神社 (長野市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-179.html Miwa Shrine] (National Zenkoji Association) {{citation needed|ja|美和神社|version=103356498|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Miwa Shrine (長野市)]] [[ja:美和神社]] {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|version=102536211|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608851]] Wikidata name: Miwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Miwa Shrine (美和神社) |image = File:美和神社 (長野市) 三ツ鳥居.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 |位置 = {{Coord|36|39|47.04|N|138|12|11.72|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=美和神社}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} |神体 = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]] |別名 = |札所等 = [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]] Seven Shrines |festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]] (Spring Grand Festival)
[[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]] (Autumn Grand Festival) |神事 = New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Miwa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=256|relief=1}} |prefecture = {{ill|Miwa|qid=Q11357343}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.66305555555556|N|138.20325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the Miwa area of Nagano City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} whose former Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also one of the seven Zenkoji shrines. == Overview == [[File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿斜め.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] It stands in front of a station in a residential area near the urban center of Nagano City, yet the shrine grounds remain quiet. A distinctive {{ill|Three Torii|ja|三ツ鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Torii|qq=|1=mitsutorii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354046}} (also known as {{ill|Miwa Torii|ja|三輪鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) marks the entrance, and nearly one hundred stone shrines (the precinct’s “Hyakusessha”) line the grounds. The New Year's Eve Festival, during which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in these stone monuments, is a singular {{ill|Special Ritual|ja|特殊神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} unlike any other. Festivals are held in the seven districts of Hongo, Ainomihigashi, Ainominishi, Yokoyama, Yodogahashi, Miwatanamachi, and Araya. == Deity == ; Main Deity * '''{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}''' (Ōmononushi-no-mikoto) :: It is identical with the deity of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]. It represents the {{ill|Japanese Spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and is said to be the father of Suiginami, the deity of {{ill|Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387216}}.‘Japan's Gods’ – Miwa Shrine entry. ; Associated Deities * {{ill|Kunari-hime|ja|国業比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunari-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kunari-himeno-kami) – said to be the mother of Ōmononushi.  Note: The mother of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], who is equated with Ōmononushi, is {{ill|Sashinakowakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinakowakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kamihato-no-kami|ja|神部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihato-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – also known as {{ill|Kamifuku-no-kami|ja|神服部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamifuku-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (read: kamihatori-no-kami).“Kamibukami”: as noted on the shrine’s bulletin board; “Kamifukami”: according to ‘Japan's Gods’ and ‘Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ – Miwa Shrine entry. == History == === Historical Overview === Its founding is unknown. Both the deity and the shrine’s name are identical to those of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], making it a shrine of the Miwa clan; however, its origins remain unclear. According to {{ill|Zenkoji Engi|ja|善光寺縁起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Engi|qq=|1=Zenkoji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116897988}}, it is recorded that Miwa Tokimaru, originally from Yamato Miwa, once visited Zenkoji and remained here, dedicating the sacred object of Ōmiwa Shrine at that time – which is why Ōmiwa Shrine does not possess a sacred object. This legend is also interpreted as evidence that members of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} migrated to this area; nearby there are sites known as Tokimaru’s Mound and Tokimaru-ji. Furthermore, pottery excavated from the {{ill|Matsunoyama Kiln Site|ja|松ノ山窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoyama Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Tanonoguchi, Shingara-cho, Nagano City, exhibits morphological and technical similarities to that from the {{ill|Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|ja|陶邑窯跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|qq=|1=Suemura Kiln Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658478}} in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]]. (This pottery is compared with the TK47 style—up to the MT15 style—dating it to the early 6th century according to the Izumi Tōyū Kiln chronicle.) As the Tōyū kilns are thought to have been operated by immigrants ([[:en:Toraijin|Immigrants]]) under the Miwa clan, this further suggests that the Miwa clan may have migrated to Nagano City.Yūki Mizoguchi, ‘Japan’s Ancient Regions and Social Integration’ (Yoshikawa Kōbun, 2015, On-Demand Edition, ISBN 9784642746182)Ken Kirihara, ‘My Notes on Ancient Studies’ (Shingai Shoseki Publishing Center, 1983) In literature, the earliest record appears in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), on February 7, when the associated deity Kunari-hime was granted a divine Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth ([[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Divine Rank]]) as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In Jōgan 8 ([[:en:866|866]]), to avert the calamity of an epidemic, an offering and sutra chanting was performed to the Sanwa deity and the Kamihato-no-kami.“Shingi-kan Sōgen. In Shinano Province, Suinai District, the Sanwa and the two Kamibu deities possessed a wrathful spirit that could cause epidemics. The provincial official and lecturer performed devout purification and offering, reciting a thousand volumes of the Diamond Sutra and ten thousand volumes of the Heart Sutra to appease the deities’ anger and avert the epidemic.” ([http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html ‘Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku’, complete edition Asahi Shimbun version]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}} ([http://www.j-texts.com/ J-TEXTS Japanese Electronic Library]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}})). This indicates the importance of the deity in the region. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as “Shinano Province, {{ill|Suinai District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinai District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, Miwa Shrine” and classifies it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Its former name was “Miwa Shrine” (三輪神社), but in An'ei 3 ([[:en:1774|1774]]), permission was granted by the Yoshida family to change its name to the current “Miwa Shrine”. In Kansei 5 ([[:en:1793|1793]]), the current shrine building was constructed. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6, [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1935 (Showa 10, [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]) it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1986 (Showa 61), it was designated as a shrine for offering envoys. === Divine Rank === * On February 7 in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), the associated deity Kunari-hime was conferred a Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth (recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the first appearance in literature. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine building was rebuilt in Kansei 5 (1793). File:美和神社 (長野市) 本殿.JPG|The upper structure of the main hall
The main hall is a single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]]. File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden (Worship Hall) * Norito Hall * Shrine Office == Auxiliary Shrines == File:美和神社 (長野市) 青麻神社.JPG|{{ill|Aoso Shrine|ja|青麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoso Shrine|qq=}}
During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], Tadaemon Osuga, a local resident, enshrined it in hopes of a speedy recovery from {{ill|Apoplexy|ja|中風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apoplexy|qq=}}. File:美和神社 (長野市) 境内の石祠群.JPG|Stone shrines of the precinct’s Hyakusessha
Along the boundary wall, stone shrines enshrining the nation’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] are arrayed, colloquially known as “Hyakusessha.” == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Exorcism Festival ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Spring Grand Festival ([[:en:April 27|April 27]]) * Wind Festival ([[:en:September 1|September 1]]) * Autumn Grand Festival ([[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]]) * Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) ([[:en:November 27|November 27]]) * New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) – A special ritual in which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in the stone shrines regarded as the nation’s Ichinomiya. * Ōharae (Grand Purification) ([[:en:December 31|December 31]]) == Local Information == ; Location * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 ; Transportation Access * Nearest Station: [[:en:Nagano Electric Railway|Nagano Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nagano Line|ja|長野電鉄長野線|lt=Nagano Line|lt_ja=長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Line|qq=|1=Nagano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8579636}}, [[:en:Hongō Station (Nagano)|Hongo Station (Nagano Prefecture)]] (a short walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine Grounds Information Board * ‘The System of Japanese Historical Place Names: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) – Miwa Shrine entry (Nagano City) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, ‘The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano’ ({{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}) – Miwa Shrine entry === Related Articles === ; Other Zenkoji Seven Shrines * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398887}} * {{ill|Kiryu Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Shrine|qq=|1=Kitome Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519306}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=|1=Yanagihara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534153}} (Sasayaki Shrine) [[ja:美和神社 (長野市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-179.html Miwa Shrine] (National Zenkoji Association) {{citation needed|ja|美和神社|version=103356498|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Miwa Shrine (長野市)]] [[ja:美和神社]] {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|version=102536211|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]] |別名 = |札所等 = [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]] Seven Shrines |festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]] (Spring Grand Festival)
[[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]] (Autumn Grand Festival) |神事 = New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Miwa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=256|relief=1}} |prefecture = {{ill|Miwa|qid=Q11357343}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.66305555555556|N|138.20325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the Miwa area of Nagano City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} whose former Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also one of the seven Zenkoji shrines. == Overview == [[File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿斜め.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] It stands in front of a station in a residential area near the urban center of Nagano City, yet the shrine grounds remain quiet. A distinctive {{ill|Three Torii|ja|三ツ鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Torii|qq=|1=mitsutorii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354046}} (also known as {{ill|Miwa Torii|ja|三輪鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) marks the entrance, and nearly one hundred stone shrines (the precinct’s “Hyakusessha”) line the grounds. The New Year's Eve Festival, during which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in these stone monuments, is a singular {{ill|Special Ritual|ja|特殊神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} unlike any other. Festivals are held in the seven districts of Hongo, Ainomihigashi, Ainominishi, Yokoyama, Yodogahashi, Miwatanamachi, and Araya. == Deity == ; Main Deity * '''{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}''' (Ōmononushi-no-mikoto) :: It is identical with the deity of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]. It represents the {{ill|Japanese Spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and is said to be the father of Suiginami, the deity of {{ill|Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387216}}.‘Japan's Gods’ – Miwa Shrine entry. ; Associated Deities * {{ill|Kunari-hime|ja|国業比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunari-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kunari-himeno-kami) – said to be the mother of Ōmononushi.  Note: The mother of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], who is equated with Ōmononushi, is {{ill|Sashinakowakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinakowakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kamihato-no-kami|ja|神部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihato-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – also known as {{ill|Kamifuku-no-kami|ja|神服部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamifuku-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (read: kamihatori-no-kami).“Kamibukami”: as noted on the shrine’s bulletin board; “Kamifukami”: according to ‘Japan's Gods’ and ‘Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ – Miwa Shrine entry. == History == === Historical Overview === Its founding is unknown. Both the deity and the shrine’s name are identical to those of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], making it a shrine of the Miwa clan; however, its origins remain unclear. According to {{ill|Zenkoji Engi|ja|善光寺縁起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Engi|qq=|1=Zenkoji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116897988}}, it is recorded that Miwa Tokimaru, originally from Yamato Miwa, once visited Zenkoji and remained here, dedicating the sacred object of Ōmiwa Shrine at that time – which is why Ōmiwa Shrine does not possess a sacred object. This legend is also interpreted as evidence that members of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} migrated to this area; nearby there are sites known as Tokimaru’s Mound and Tokimaru-ji. Furthermore, pottery excavated from the {{ill|Matsunoyama Kiln Site|ja|松ノ山窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoyama Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Tanonoguchi, Shingara-cho, Nagano City, exhibits morphological and technical similarities to that from the {{ill|Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|ja|陶邑窯跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|qq=|1=Suemura Kiln Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658478}} in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]]. (This pottery is compared with the TK47 style—up to the MT15 style—dating it to the early 6th century according to the Izumi Tōyū Kiln chronicle.) As the Tōyū kilns are thought to have been operated by immigrants ([[:en:Toraijin|Immigrants]]) under the Miwa clan, this further suggests that the Miwa clan may have migrated to Nagano City.Yūki Mizoguchi, ‘Japan’s Ancient Regions and Social Integration’ (Yoshikawa Kōbun, 2015, On-Demand Edition, ISBN 9784642746182)Ken Kirihara, ‘My Notes on Ancient Studies’ (Shingai Shoseki Publishing Center, 1983) In literature, the earliest record appears in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), on February 7, when the associated deity Kunari-hime was granted a divine Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth ([[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Divine Rank]]) as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In Jōgan 8 ([[:en:866|866]]), to avert the calamity of an epidemic, an offering and sutra chanting was performed to the Sanwa deity and the Kamihato-no-kami.“Shingi-kan Sōgen. In Shinano Province, Suinai District, the Sanwa and the two Kamibu deities possessed a wrathful spirit that could cause epidemics. The provincial official and lecturer performed devout purification and offering, reciting a thousand volumes of the Diamond Sutra and ten thousand volumes of the Heart Sutra to appease the deities’ anger and avert the epidemic.” ([http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html ‘Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku’, complete edition Asahi Shimbun version]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}} ([http://www.j-texts.com/ J-TEXTS Japanese Electronic Library]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}})). This indicates the importance of the deity in the region. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as “Shinano Province, {{ill|Suinai District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinai District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, Miwa Shrine” and classifies it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Its former name was “Miwa Shrine” (三輪神社), but in An'ei 3 ([[:en:1774|1774]]), permission was granted by the Yoshida family to change its name to the current “Miwa Shrine”. In Kansei 5 ([[:en:1793|1793]]), the current shrine building was constructed. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6, [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1935 (Showa 10, [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]) it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1986 (Showa 61), it was designated as a shrine for offering envoys. === Divine Rank === * On February 7 in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), the associated deity Kunari-hime was conferred a Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth (recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the first appearance in literature. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine building was rebuilt in Kansei 5 (1793). File:美和神社 (長野市) 本殿.JPG|The upper structure of the main hall
The main hall is a single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]]. File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden (Worship Hall) * Norito Hall * Shrine Office == Auxiliary Shrines == File:美和神社 (長野市) 青麻神社.JPG|{{ill|Aoso Shrine|ja|青麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoso Shrine|qq=}}
During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], Tadaemon Osuga, a local resident, enshrined it in hopes of a speedy recovery from {{ill|Apoplexy|ja|中風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apoplexy|qq=}}. File:美和神社 (長野市) 境内の石祠群.JPG|Stone shrines of the precinct’s Hyakusessha
Along the boundary wall, stone shrines enshrining the nation’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] are arrayed, colloquially known as “Hyakusessha.” == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Exorcism Festival ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Spring Grand Festival ([[:en:April 27|April 27]]) * Wind Festival ([[:en:September 1|September 1]]) * Autumn Grand Festival ([[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]]) * Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) ([[:en:November 27|November 27]]) * New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) – A special ritual in which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in the stone shrines regarded as the nation’s Ichinomiya. * Ōharae (Grand Purification) ([[:en:December 31|December 31]]) == Local Information == ; Location * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 ; Transportation Access * Nearest Station: [[:en:Nagano Electric Railway|Nagano Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nagano Line|ja|長野電鉄長野線|lt=Nagano Line|lt_ja=長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Line|qq=|1=Nagano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8579636}}, [[:en:Hongō Station (Nagano)|Hongo Station (Nagano Prefecture)]] (a short walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine Grounds Information Board * ‘The System of Japanese Historical Place Names: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) – Miwa Shrine entry (Nagano City) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, ‘The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano’ ({{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}) – Miwa Shrine entry === Related Articles === ; Other Zenkoji Seven Shrines * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398887}} * {{ill|Kiryu Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Shrine|qq=|1=Kitome Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519306}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=|1=Yanagihara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534153}} (Sasayaki Shrine) [[ja:美和神社 (長野市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-179.html Miwa Shrine] (National Zenkoji Association) {{citation needed|ja|美和神社|version=103356498|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Miwa Shrine (長野市)]] [[ja:美和神社]] {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|version=102536211|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608851]] Wikidata name: Miwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Miwa Shrine (美和神社) |image = File:美和神社 (長野市) 三ツ鳥居.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 |位置 = {{Coord|36|39|47.04|N|138|12|11.72|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=美和神社}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} |神体 = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]] |別名 = |札所等 = [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]] Seven Shrines |festival = [[:en:April 27|April 27]] (Spring Grand Festival)
[[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]] (Autumn Grand Festival) |神事 = New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano Prefecture|label=Miwa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=256|relief=1}} |prefecture = {{ill|Miwa|qid=Q11357343}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.66305555555556|N|138.20325|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (美和神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the Miwa area of Nagano City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} whose former Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was that of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also one of the seven Zenkoji shrines. == Overview == [[File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿斜め.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] It stands in front of a station in a residential area near the urban center of Nagano City, yet the shrine grounds remain quiet. A distinctive {{ill|Three Torii|ja|三ツ鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Torii|qq=|1=mitsutorii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354046}} (also known as {{ill|Miwa Torii|ja|三輪鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) marks the entrance, and nearly one hundred stone shrines (the precinct’s “Hyakusessha”) line the grounds. The New Year's Eve Festival, during which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in these stone monuments, is a singular {{ill|Special Ritual|ja|特殊神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} unlike any other. Festivals are held in the seven districts of Hongo, Ainomihigashi, Ainominishi, Yokoyama, Yodogahashi, Miwatanamachi, and Araya. == Deity == ; Main Deity * '''{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}''' (Ōmononushi-no-mikoto) :: It is identical with the deity of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]. It represents the {{ill|Japanese Spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and is said to be the father of Suiginami, the deity of {{ill|Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|ja|健御名方富命彦神別神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take-minakata Tomifumihiko no Miketsu Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto Hikokamiwake Shrine (Nagano City Hako Shimizu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387216}}.‘Japan's Gods’ – Miwa Shrine entry. ; Associated Deities * {{ill|Kunari-hime|ja|国業比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunari-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kunari-himeno-kami) – said to be the mother of Ōmononushi.  Note: The mother of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], who is equated with Ōmononushi, is {{ill|Sashinakowakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashinakowakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kamihato-no-kami|ja|神部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihato-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – also known as {{ill|Kamifuku-no-kami|ja|神服部神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamifuku-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (read: kamihatori-no-kami).“Kamibukami”: as noted on the shrine’s bulletin board; “Kamifukami”: according to ‘Japan's Gods’ and ‘Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ – Miwa Shrine entry. == History == === Historical Overview === Its founding is unknown. Both the deity and the shrine’s name are identical to those of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], making it a shrine of the Miwa clan; however, its origins remain unclear. According to {{ill|Zenkoji Engi|ja|善光寺縁起|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkoji Engi|qq=|1=Zenkoji Engi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116897988}}, it is recorded that Miwa Tokimaru, originally from Yamato Miwa, once visited Zenkoji and remained here, dedicating the sacred object of Ōmiwa Shrine at that time – which is why Ōmiwa Shrine does not possess a sacred object. This legend is also interpreted as evidence that members of the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} migrated to this area; nearby there are sites known as Tokimaru’s Mound and Tokimaru-ji. Furthermore, pottery excavated from the {{ill|Matsunoyama Kiln Site|ja|松ノ山窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoyama Kiln Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Tanonoguchi, Shingara-cho, Nagano City, exhibits morphological and technical similarities to that from the {{ill|Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|ja|陶邑窯跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyū Kiln Site Cluster|qq=|1=Suemura Kiln Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658478}} in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]]. (This pottery is compared with the TK47 style—up to the MT15 style—dating it to the early 6th century according to the Izumi Tōyū Kiln chronicle.) As the Tōyū kilns are thought to have been operated by immigrants ([[:en:Toraijin|Immigrants]]) under the Miwa clan, this further suggests that the Miwa clan may have migrated to Nagano City.Yūki Mizoguchi, ‘Japan’s Ancient Regions and Social Integration’ (Yoshikawa Kōbun, 2015, On-Demand Edition, ISBN 9784642746182)Ken Kirihara, ‘My Notes on Ancient Studies’ (Shingai Shoseki Publishing Center, 1983) In literature, the earliest record appears in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), on February 7, when the associated deity Kunari-hime was granted a divine Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth ([[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Divine Rank]]) as recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]. In Jōgan 8 ([[:en:866|866]]), to avert the calamity of an epidemic, an offering and sutra chanting was performed to the Sanwa deity and the Kamihato-no-kami.“Shingi-kan Sōgen. In Shinano Province, Suinai District, the Sanwa and the two Kamibu deities possessed a wrathful spirit that could cause epidemics. The provincial official and lecturer performed devout purification and offering, reciting a thousand volumes of the Diamond Sutra and ten thousand volumes of the Heart Sutra to appease the deities’ anger and avert the epidemic.” ([http://www.j-texts.com/chuko/sandai.html ‘Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku’, complete edition Asahi Shimbun version]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}} ([http://www.j-texts.com/ J-TEXTS Japanese Electronic Library]{{リンク切れ|date=2023rd year 2月}})). This indicates the importance of the deity in the region. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as “Shinano Province, {{ill|Suinai District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suinai District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, Miwa Shrine” and classifies it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Its former name was “Miwa Shrine” (三輪神社), but in An'ei 3 ([[:en:1774|1774]]), permission was granted by the Yoshida family to change its name to the current “Miwa Shrine”. In Kansei 5 ([[:en:1793|1793]]), the current shrine building was constructed. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6, [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1935 (Showa 10, [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]) it was elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1986 (Showa 61), it was designated as a shrine for offering envoys. === Divine Rank === * On February 7 in Jōgan 3 ([[:en:861|861]]), the associated deity Kunari-hime was conferred a Rank from Upper Sixth to Lower Fifth (recorded in [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) – the first appearance in literature. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine building was rebuilt in Kansei 5 (1793). File:美和神社 (長野市) 本殿.JPG|The upper structure of the main hall
The main hall is a single-bay shrine in [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryu-zō style]]. File:美和神社 (長野市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden (Worship Hall) * Norito Hall * Shrine Office == Auxiliary Shrines == File:美和神社 (長野市) 青麻神社.JPG|{{ill|Aoso Shrine|ja|青麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoso Shrine|qq=}}
During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], Tadaemon Osuga, a local resident, enshrined it in hopes of a speedy recovery from {{ill|Apoplexy|ja|中風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apoplexy|qq=}}. File:美和神社 (長野市) 境内の石祠群.JPG|Stone shrines of the precinct’s Hyakusessha
Along the boundary wall, stone shrines enshrining the nation’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] are arrayed, colloquially known as “Hyakusessha.” == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Exorcism Festival ([[:en:February 3|February 3]]) * Spring Grand Festival ([[:en:April 27|April 27]]) * Wind Festival ([[:en:September 1|September 1]]) * Autumn Grand Festival ([[:en:September 26|September 26]] ・ [[:en:September 27|27]]) * Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) ([[:en:November 27|November 27]]) * New Year's Eve Festival ([[:en:December 16|December 16]]) – A special ritual in which offerings are presented to the deities enshrined in the stone shrines regarded as the nation’s Ichinomiya. * Ōharae (Grand Purification) ([[:en:December 31|December 31]]) == Local Information == ; Location * [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Oaza {{ill|Miwa (Nagano City)|ja|三輪 (長野市)|lt=Miwa|lt_ja=三輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa (Nagano City)|qq=|1=Miwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357343}} Aza Aino-moku Higashi 514 ; Transportation Access * Nearest Station: [[:en:Nagano Electric Railway|Nagano Electric Railway]] {{ill|Nagano Line|ja|長野電鉄長野線|lt=Nagano Line|lt_ja=長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Line|qq=|1=Nagano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8579636}}, [[:en:Hongō Station (Nagano)|Hongo Station (Nagano Prefecture)]] (a short walk) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine Grounds Information Board * ‘The System of Japanese Historical Place Names: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture’ ([[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]) – Miwa Shrine entry (Nagano City) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, ‘The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano’ ({{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|qq=|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}) – Miwa Shrine entry === Related Articles === ; Other Zenkoji Seven Shrines * {{ill|Yufuku Shrine|ja|湯福神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuku Shrine|qq=|1=Yubuku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563772}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Takei Shrine|ja|武井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Tsumashina Shrine|ja|妻科神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumashina Shrine|qq=}} – One of Zenkoji’s Three Guardian Shrines (Zenkoji Three Shrines) * {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|ja|加茂神社 (長野市西長野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Nagano City, Nishi-Nagano)|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398887}} * {{ill|Kiryu Shrine|ja|木留神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu Shrine|qq=|1=Kitome Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519306}} * {{ill|Yanagihara Shrine|ja|柳原神社 (長野市)|lt=Yanagihara Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Shrine|qq=|1=Yanagihara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11534153}} (Sasayaki Shrine) [[ja:美和神社 (長野市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.zenkojikai.com/shinetsu/s-179.html Miwa Shrine] (National Zenkoji Association) {{citation needed|ja|美和神社|version=103356498|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Miwa Shrine (長野市)]] [[ja:美和神社]] {{translated page|ja|美和神社 (長野市)|version=102536211|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miwa Shrine (Nagano)]][[Category:qq]]
